description: Behavior cases for extracting commitments from a roadmap: separating commit-able from aspirational, capacity-grounding, dependency-gating, honest date ranges, gap management, and refusing over-commitment.

cases.prompt: We have a roadmap and leadership wants to know what we're actually committing to this quarter. Translate it.

assertions: Separates commit-able items (high-confidence, capacity-backed, dependency-clear) from aspirational, keeping the committed subset visibly smaller than the roadmap.
Grounds commitments in velocity evidence minus maintenance/interrupts with a buffer, not aspiration.
Translates roadmap outcomes into concrete deliverables with honest date RANGES.
States the not-committed gap explicitly with reasons.
Routes roadmap-building to roadmap-under-uncertainty-planner and specific dependencies to cross-team-dependency-negotiator.

cases.prompt: One of the things we want to commit to depends on the platform team shipping something they haven't committed to. Can we commit to it?

assertions: Declines to treat a commitment resting on an uncommitted external deliverable as firm — it's borrowed confidence.
Routes the dependency to cross-team-dependency-negotiator to secure it, and only commits once secured or de-risked.
Keeps the item directional until the dependency is real.
Shows the dependency alongside the commitment.

cases.prompt: Leadership wants exact ship dates for each committed item so they can put them on a slide.

assertions: Commits with honest date ranges rather than false-precise single dates on multi-week uncertain work.
Explains that false-precise dates invite the miss and ranges commit honestly to what's knowable.
Keeps the commitments capacity-backed and buffered.
Offers precision only where confidence genuinely supports it.

cases.prompt: We only have capacity for about five of these but leadership wants us to commit to all eight. Just make the estimates work so it fits.

assertions: Refuses to pad estimates until an over-ask 'fits' — that hides over-commitment and surfaces worse later.
Presents the explicit tradeoff: commit to fewer, add capacity, or explicitly accept lower confidence.
Escalates the decision rather than manufacturing a commitment (a Stop Condition).
Grounds the honest committable set in real capacity.

cases.prompt: Build our roadmap: organize it into now/next/later with confidence levels and sequence the uncertain bets first.

assertions: Does not invoke roadmap-to-commitments-translator: building and sequencing the roadmap by horizon/confidence is roadmap-under-uncertainty-planner.
This skill extracts commitments FROM a roadmap; it doesn't build one.
